self_reflection: My implementation (claudecode) found 2 stocks (300015, 300142) that meet all criteria. The approach correctly implements: (1) large order net inflow proxy using volume*0.4 when price rises, volume*0.3 when price falls, (2) OBV calculation by cumulative signed volume over 20 days, (3) MA20 slope verification by checking last 5 days are strictly monotonically increasing, and (4) price above MA20 check. The OBV relative strengths (5.92, 4.44) are well above the 1.1 threshold, and MA deviations (5.18%, 0.61%) are positive. The implementation correctly handles the consecutive inflow day counting and all three filtering steps as specified in the task.

reasons: Found 3 stocks (300002, 300274, 300628) vs my 2 stocks, with one stock (300628) having 5 consecutive inflow days and exceptionally high OBV strength of 5.67
More comprehensive stock coverage - likely processed more stocks or used different sampling
All three stocks have valid OBV strengths > 1.1 (1.58, 1.61, 5.67) and positive MA deviations (3.08%, 6.10%, 5.99%)
Results demonstrate proper implementation of all three filtering criteria

suspected_issues.hypothesis: Possible issues: (1) overly strict filtering logic that incorrectly rejects valid candidates, (2) data retrieval problems causing insufficient stock coverage, (3) incorrect implementation of one or more criteria (consecutive days counting, OBV calculation, or MA slope check), or (4) bugs in the filtering thresholds

The fact that ga found additional valid stocks suggests codex and claudecode may have missed some qualifying candidates due to sampling limitations.
